name: blender-mcp
title: Blender 3D
description: Blender 3D modelling via TCP socket addon — object CRUD, materials, scene inspection, code execution, screenshot capture
author: ahujasid

highlights.text: Objects — create, modify, delete meshes, lights, cameras
Materials — assign and edit PBR materials and textures
Scene inspection — list objects, hierarchy, transforms
Screenshots — capture viewport for visual feedback
Code execution — run arbitrary Blender Python scripts
Asset libraries — Poly Haven, Hyper3D Rodin, Sketchfab

readme:
### What it does

Blender MCP connects your AI agent to a running Blender instance via a TCP socket addon. Your agent can create and manipulate 3D objects, assign materials, inspect the scene graph, execute Python scripts, and capture viewport screenshots for visual feedback.

Describe what you want to build in natural language and the agent translates it into Blender operations — creating meshes, positioning objects, setting up lighting, and iterating based on screenshots.

### How it works

Two components work together: a Blender addon that runs a TCP socket server inside Blender, and the MCP server that bridges AI tool calls to that socket. Install the addon in Blender, start the socket server, then connect the MCP.

### Asset integrations

Optional integrations with Poly Haven (free HDRIs, textures, models), Hyper3D Rodin (AI-generated 3D models), and Sketchfab (3D model marketplace) let your agent pull in assets directly.

### Safety note

The `execute_blender_code` tool runs arbitrary Python inside Blender. This is the expected pattern for addon-based MCPs but means the agent has full scripting access to your Blender session. No write gates — all operations execute immediately.

scenarios.label: Build a scene from a description
scenarios.body: Describe a 3D scene in natural language. The agent creates objects, positions them, assigns materials, sets up lighting, then captures a screenshot for review and iteration.

scenarios.label: Iterate on a model with visual feedback
scenarios.body: Inspect the current scene, capture a viewport screenshot, discuss changes, then execute modifications and capture again to compare before and after.

scenarios.label: Script a batch operation
scenarios.body: Write and execute a Blender Python script to batch-process objects — rename, reposition, apply modifiers, or export selected meshes to a target format.

env.name: BLENDER_PORT
env.description: TCP port for Blender addon socket server

setup.help_text: Install the Blender MCP addon: download from the repository, then in Blender → Edit → Preferences → Add-ons → Install. Enable the addon and start the socket server. The MCP connects via TCP on the configured port.
prerequisites: Blender MCP addon installed and enabled
Addon socket server running in Blender

certification.security_notes: Executes arbitrary Python code inside Blender via execute_blender_code tool — expected for addon pattern. No network exfil beyond optional Poly Haven / Sketchfab integrations.

notes: Dominant Blender MCP (18k+ stars). Requires Blender addon installed separately. Two-component: addon runs TCP socket server inside Blender, MCP server bridges to it. Supports Poly Haven, Hyper3D Rodin, Sketchfab integrations.
